checks.a_analysis: The output contains standard headings and text that correspond directly to the source document. No invented section headings or hallucinated body text were identified beyond the expected AI additions such as alt-text.

The output includes the URL 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)' which correctly reflects the text in the source document on page 7. No other contact information or URLs were present in the source.
All pages and major sections of the source document are represented in the output document structure. While some sections are truncated (evaluated under C5), the sections themselves are present.
The primary tables (Agenda Topic table, Attendance tables, Minutes Vote table, and PSAP Deployment table) all contain the correct number of rows corresponding to the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to include nested list items and critical data points in the supplemental notes sections (pages 6, 7, and 8). Specifically, sub-bullets under 'Military Sites Update' and 'Cybersecurity Remediation' on page 6 are missing. On page 7, the detailed error counts and readiness percentages for specific counties (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk) under the 'GIS Update' are omitted, as are the population and land mass statistics. On page 8, the specific staffing detail for the NMAC update is missing. These cumulative omissions represent a significant loss of informational detail from the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: 'Seymour Johnson AFB is working on a multi year contract... regarding Camp Lejune'; Page 7: 'Cleveland County and Kings Mountain – not scheduled (361 critical errors/73.34%) ... Polk County – Q3 migration (561 critical errors/84.61%)'; Page 7: 'Total Population served by NG 911: 10,477,959 (99%). Total land mass covered by NG911: 7,328.7 sq. mi. (96%).'; Page 8: 'Tier 2 technician selection completed and onboarding now.'
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding sections in the output (Sections 6, 7, and 8) contain only the top-level headings and list items, with no sub-bullets or data points included.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are two minor meaning-altering differences. First, in the Ethics Statement, the legal statute 'G.S. 138A-15' was incorrectly converted to 'G.S. 138-15'. Second, in the Topic table for the June 9 agenda, the output incorrectly assigns all sub-topics (a through e) under topic 6 to 'Greg Dotson' as the presenter, whereas the source assigns only sub-topic 'e' to him, with topics 6 and 6a-d belonging to 'Tom Rogers'.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. 'G.S. 138A-15' (Page 5); 2. Topic 6 sub-topics a-d are under the presenter 'Tom Rogers' (Page 1).
checks.b_evidence_output: 1. 'G.S. 138-15' (Section 5, element index 29); 2. Presenter 'Greg Dotson' is assigned to the combined cell for sub-topics a-e (Section 1, table element 6, row 7).
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 5, element index 29; Section 1, table row 7

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output document and the source PDF for duplicated content. The headers such as 'North Carolina 911 Board' appear on multiple pages in the source and are correctly represented in the output as distinct headings for each section. No instances of accidental content duplication (paragraphs or list items appearing more often than in the source) were found.

The heading hierarchy is logically structured. H1 is used for major document titles (e.g., at the start of the agenda and the minutes), H2 for primary section headers, and H3 for sub-sections (e.g., 'Attendance', 'Minutes Vote', 'Next Up Migrations'). There are no skips in the hierarchy (e.g., H1 to H3). The promotion of some section titles to H2 or H1 for consistency across sections is an acceptable accessibility normalization.

There is a structural corruption in the 'Attendance' table continuation on Page 3. In the source PDF, the table continues with 4 columns. In the output (Section 2, Element 0), the table only has 3 columns. Furthermore, the first row of data (containing the guest 'Brian Eaton -- AT&T') is incorrectly tagged as a header row (`is_header_row: true`), which misrepresents the data structure. Other tables, like the Agenda table on Page 1 and the Migration tables on Page 5, are structurally sound.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table on Page 3 continues with guest names across 4 columns (Name, Phone/Teams, In-Person, Absent).
checks.b_evidence_output: The table in Section 2, Element 0 has only 3 columns and incorrectly marks the first row ('Brian Eaton -- AT&T') as a header row.
checks.b_evidence_location: Attendance table continuation, Page 3 / Section 2, Element index 0

checks.a_analysis: The output document follows the logical reading order of the source PDF, progressing from the initial agenda to the previous minutes and then into the current meeting updates.

Several lists in the output document have been flattened, resulting in the loss of sub-bullet nesting and associated content. On Page 6 (Section 5, Element 3), the sub-bullets for 'Military Sites Update' and 'Cybersecurity Remediation' are entirely missing. On Page 7 (Section 6, Element 1), sub-bullets under '4 jurisdictions remain...', 'RFAI PSAPs...', and 'Statistics' are also missing. This is a major structural failure as significant descriptive information is omitted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: 'Military Sites Update' has sub-bullets like 'Seymour Johnson AFB...'. 'Cybersecurity Remediation' has sub-bullets like 'Ad hoc committee...'. Page 7: 'GIS Update' has sub-bullets for jurisdictions (Cleveland, Lincoln, etc.) and Statistics.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 5, Element 3 and Section 6, Element 1 show flat lists where these sub-bullets and their content are absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: Additional project notes (Page 6 / Section 5, Element 3) and GIS Update (Page 7 / Section 6, Element 1)

checks.a_analysis: Two significant links present on Page 7 of the source PDF are entirely missing from the output. These are the link to the 'NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage' and the ArcGIS dashboard link 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)'. The output does not contain the text or the link structures for these elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage; NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)
checks.b_evidence_output: The text and links are missing from Section 6.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the original PDF. The agenda from page 1, the attendance list and minutes from pages 2-4, and the updates from pages 5-8 are all presented in the correct sequence. Responsive adjustments, such as converting the tab-spaced agenda into a formal table, improve clarity without altering the document's intent. Header information and sectioning are consistently applied.

All tables from the source PDF are accurately rendered in the HTML. This includes the 'Attendance' table (with its complex sub-grouping), the 'Minutes Vote' table, the 'Next Up Migrations' table, and the 'Current Deployment Service Types' table. Row and column relationships are preserved, and cell contents are clearly aligned and readable. Header rows are visually distinct.

Meaningful images are present in the HTML output. This includes the primary agency logos (NC DIT 911 Board) appearing at the start of various sections as they did in the PDF pages, and the bar chart 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' on page 6. Decorative elements like the 'DRAFT' watermark are correctly omitted per accessibility standards.

There is no visual duplication of body content (paragraphs, tables, or lists). While the agency logo and certain headers repeat, this accurately reflects their appearance at the top of multiple pages in the source PDF. The document title and section headings appearing in the navigation menu and document body is an intentional accessibility feature and not considered content duplication.
